About.Name: Fatimah Mohammed
About.AboutText: Hi, I am Fatimah Mohammed. I am an Electrical Engineering student at Seattle Pacific University with interests in circuit design, PCB development, MATLAB, LTspice, and programming. I enjoy building hands-on engineering projects and combining hardware with technical problem-solving.

Experience.ExperienceText: I am currently pursuing Electrical Engineering at Seattle Pacific University while building experience through circuit analysis, simulation, and design projects. My work has included active filter design, MATLAB analysis, LTspice simulation, and technical documentation.
Experience.EducationText: My education at Seattle Pacific University has given me experience in electronics, circuit analysis, engineering design, and programming. Through labs and projects, I have developed stronger skills in both theoretical and hands-on problem solving.
Experience.EmploymentText: In addition to coursework, I have worked on technical assignments and engineering projects involving circuit design, simulation, troubleshooting, and report writing.
Experience.SkillsText: My technical skills include circuit design and simulation, MATLAB analysis, LTspice modeling, and programming in C++, JavaScript, Node.js, HTML, and CSS.

Project1.Title: 4th-Order Sallen-Key Bandpass Filter Design
Project1.Desc:
Designed and analyzed a 4th-order active bandpass filter centered at 25 kHz using two cascaded Sallen-Key stages. Verified transfer function behavior in MATLAB and evaluated gain at the desired pass frequency and rejected frequencies using Bode plots and simulation screenshots.

This project demonstrates active filter design, frequency response analysis, and practical circuit implementation.
